title: Rtp Fallback Channel Switching
description: A payments-and-settlement workflow that controls release, routing, and execution timing across multiple rails and liquidity constraints. Focus case: rtp fallback channel switching.
assumptions: Channels and rails declared available in the initial state reflect real-time availability at workflow start.
Reservations (liquidity_reserve_token) are atomic relative to the payment_case lifecycle and prevent double allocation.
Routing policy objects (timeout_profile, retry_policy) encode deterministic decision logic used by actions.
Settlement windows are mutually exclusive resources that must be reserved before execution binding.
External switch and credential exchanges succeed or are reverted via explicit release actions in the model.
risks: Liquidity shortfall: reserved funds may be insufficient at execution time, causing failed settlement.
Race conditions between parallel assignments and releases of rails/channels leading to inconsistent allocation.
Stale routing decisions: delay between assignment and execution can render the chosen rail unavailable.
Message loss or credential expiry during channel authentication resulting in aborted transitions.
Regulatory or compliance constraints not modelled (e.g., sanctions screening) could block settlement.
